name: Claiborne, H. Bishop
motto: "And wise men say nothing in dangerous times."
description: He is a "stumpage" student. He can tell an interesting story of the first few days of the first term of the Teachers' College. He is that dependable, fine type of student that helped to build the reputation of the College. Since he received his diploma in 1915, Bishop has been a successful teacher.
text:
"And wise men say nothing in dangerous times."
He is a "stumpage" student. He can tell an interesting story of the first few days of the first term of the Teachers' College. He is that dependable, fine type of student that helped to build the reputation of the College. Since he received his diploma in 1915, Bishop has been a successful teacher.

name: Clyde, Williams
motto: "And her 'yes'; once said to you, Shall be 'yes' forevermore."
text: "And her 'yes'; once said to you, Shall be 'yes' forevermore."

name: D. O., Hamil
description: He is familiarly called, hails from the good county of Leake, where it was formerly said no bad failures and railroad wrecks ever occurred. D. O. is energetic and always "on the job." He has been our repair man for these three years. We predict for him success in his field of labor and that he will continue to repair the defective and broken places around him. His interest in the welfare of the College and his fellow associates is demonstrated in that he allied himself with the following College activities:

name: Hobby, Marvin Pruitt
motto: "A lovely smiling, always gay, That's Hobby in every way."
clubs: President Nashoba and Newton County Club
description: "Red" is endowed with an equal amount of seriousness and humor, and if he takes his troubles out in the teaching profession as stoically as he has done those of his love affairs (up to the beginning of this year), we feel sure in guaranteeing him a bright future. He will be a good "Hobby" for anyone to have.
text:
"A lovely smiling, always gay, That's Hobby in every way."
"Red" is endowed with an equal amount of seriousness and humor, and if he takes his troubles out in the teaching profession as stoically as he has done those of his love affairs (up to the beginning of this year), we feel sure in guaranteeing him a bright future. He will be a good "Hobby" for anyone to have.

name: Hollingsworth, Wilma
motto: "For she is wise, if I can judge her, And fair she is, if that mine eyes be true, And true she is, as she hath proved herself."
text: "For she is wise, if I can judge her, And fair she is, if that mine eyes be true, And true she is, as she hath proved herself."

name: Leech, Dewey C.
motto: "He meets you with a smile, Regardless of the day; To him life is worth while; Good nature guides his way."
description: No student is better known and better liked than Dewey C. Leech. His merits as a debater and leader are recognized here and elsewhere, as is shown by his record at the S. T. C., and the fact that he is representative in the State Legislature from Monroe County. Dewey meets and fights the problems of life fairly and squarely. We predict a great future for him.
text:
"He meets you with a smile, Regardless of the day; To him life is worth while; Good nature guides his way."
No student is better known and better liked than Dewey C. Leech. His merits as a debater and leader are recognized here and elsewhere, as is shown by his record at the S. T. C., and the fact that he is representative in the State Legislature from Monroe County. Dewey meets and fights the problems of life fairly and squarely. We predict a great future for him.

name: Cowart, O. E.
motto: "Give every man thine ear, but few thy voice."
description: He came to us from the country, and he possesses all the characteristics of the rural mind—dependability, slowness in forming judgments, but sureness, industry, thoroughness. He is a good athlete, and is popular with students and faculty.
text:
"Give every man thine ear, but few thy voice."
He came to us from the country, and he possesses all the characteristics of the rural mind—dependability, slowness in forming judgments, but sureness, industry, thoroughness. He is a good athlete, and is popular with students and faculty.

name: Oaks, Plume
clubs: Delegate National Convention Y. W. C. A. at New York, '24
description: The above quotation is true of Plume's college life, and so we know it will be of her future. She is a rare compound of genuineness, common sense, sympathy, and humor. She is a girl to whom a tonic likes to go in trouble and joy.
text:
"She hath done what she could."
The above quotation is true of Plume's college life, and so we know it will be of her future. She is a rare compound of genuineness, common sense, sympathy, and humor. She is a girl to whom a tonic likes to go in trouble and joy.

name: Jolly, R. E.
description: Once back in the pine hills of his native Neshoba home, after a long service in the Medical Corps of the American army during the World War, R. E. became ill with Pedagogical Fever. He thenceforth strayed forward to the Teachers' College for the cure of his ills. He has made good in college. His success and his friendships have been made through his ability to keep cool and to apply his logical reasoning at times of supreme test. He has allied himself with a number of the best college activities and has been honored with memberships and offices in the following organizations:

name: McCandless, C. H.
motto: "Efficiency is the principle of his success."
clubs: Commander American Legion Normal College Post No. 117, '23-'24
Y. M. C. A. Staff Officer, '23-'24
description: He is an orator, debater, correspondent, and leader. His counsel has been sought on many questions among the students and other friends. Congeniality, hospitality, and loyalty have won for him a place in the hearts of his fellow students.
text:
"Efficiency is the principle of his success."
He is an orator, debater, correspondent, and leader. His counsel has been sought on many questions among the students and other friends. Congeniality, hospitality, and loyalty have won for him a place in the hearts of his fellow students.

name: Spencer, Mrs. Fannie J. H.
description: How does it happen that Mrs. Spencer, who, as a bride many years ago, supposed her school days were over, after having reared four children to maturity and seeing them all married, herself a grandmother of several years' standing, is now receiving a college degree? Surely, back of these facts about Mrs. Spencer's life must be some truths more inspiring than the themes of novelists. These truths are that Mrs. Spencer has always loved knowledge for its own sake; that she never believed she was getting too old to learn; and, last but not least, she, like Napoleon, determined that there should be no Alps.
text:
"There are no Alps."
How does it happen that Mrs. Spencer, who, as a bride many years ago, supposed her school days were over, after having reared four children to maturity and seeing them all married, herself a grandmother of several years' standing, is now receiving a college degree? Surely, back of these facts about Mrs. Spencer's life must be some truths more inspiring than the themes of novelists. These truths are that Mrs. Spencer has always loved knowledge for its own sake; that she never believed she was getting too old to learn; and, last but not least, she, like Napoleon, determined that there should be no Alps.

name: Burns, L. C.
motto: "A friend is worth all hazards we can run."
description: Ask him to do something for you, and watch him do it and do it well. Put upon him the test of true friendship, and watch him prove one hundred per cent true.
text:
"A friend is worth all hazards we can run."
Ask him to do something for you, and watch him do it and do it well. Put upon him the test of true friendship, and watch him prove one hundred per cent true.

name: Cox, Levia B.
description: From out of the heart of Johns there came to us in '18 a girl with a sweet disposition. Her personality and magnetism have made for her scores of friends among the student body.
text: From out of the heart of Johns there came to us in '18 a girl with a sweet disposition. Her personality and magnetism have made for her scores of friends among the student body.

name: Cooley, E. Ralph
description: Cooley is a broad, fair-minded, true-hearted friend, liked by everyone. He is one of those quiet, good-natured, pleasing persons who are always ready to answer the call of duty, entering everything with the determination to come out successfully. We have been blessed in having him for a friend and classmate.
text: Cooley is a broad, fair-minded, true-hearted friend, liked by everyone. He is one of those quiet, good-natured, pleasing persons who are always ready to answer the call of duty, entering everything with the determination to come out successfully. We have been blessed in having him for a friend and classmate.

name: Hays, Kathleen K.
description: Fun-loving and happy, ambitious and gay, Is our own Kathleen Hays.
text: Fun-loving and happy, ambitious and gay, Is our own Kathleen Hays.

name: Velma, Shay
description: A girl she is to all the college dear, And passing rich with twenty hours a year; Remote from town she'll run her goodly race, And none can fill nor wish to fill her place; Unpracticed she to frown or seek for power, By flatteries fashioned to the present hour; For other aims her heart has learned to prize— To teach the art of sewing and making pies.
text: A girl she is to all the college dear, And passing rich with twenty hours a year; Remote from town she'll run her goodly race, And none can fill nor wish to fill her place; Unpracticed she to frown or seek for power, By flatteries fashioned to the present hour; For other aims her heart has learned to prize— To teach the art of sewing and making pies.

name: Taylor, J. T.
motto: "To make a happy fireside home For swains and wife, That's the true pathos and sublime Of human life."
description: Taylor is a family man, but, in spite of domestic cares, was a faithful student.
text:
"To make a happy fireside home For swains and wife, That's the true pathos and sublime Of human life."
Taylor is a family man, but, in spite of domestic cares, was a faithful student.

name: Brewer, Deaness
motto: "Not afraid of work, but not in sympathy with it."
description: Deaness came to us in the fall of '23; she has been a loyal student ever since. "Dean" has set herself a high goal and has the ability and determination to reach it. We feel that her accomplishments here as a student is the initial step toward her goal. Her gentle good nature and fair dealings have won for her a host of friends.
